title: Command for cancelling regular payments for a recurring BOLT12 offer
description: The **cancelrepeatpay** RPC command prevents further repeated payments for a prior **repeatpay** invocation.

The command will arrange for **repeatpay** to send a *cancel* message instead of an invoice request, next time one is due.  This cannot be undone (though you may be able to restart another sequence of payments by invoking **repeatpay** again).
Unique label to identify this recurring payment
Reason to (try to) give the issuer for why we cancelled
The offer we are paying (same as the bolt12 parameter).
The maximum millisatoshi amount we are authorized to pay per payment for the next invoice (it may change with each invoice, as we do further currency conversion).
The original currency amount specified by the amendpay (e.g. '100.00USD'), if a currency was given.
Unique label to identify this recurring payment
The transient id we have used to identify ourselves as a (repeat) payer.
The random data we use to derive our payer_id.
Current status of the recurring payment.
The reason given when this payment was cancelled.
Number of payments successfully completed so far.
Log of status transitions and notable events.

author: Rusty Russell [rusty@rustcorp.com.au](mailto:rusty@rustcorp.com.au) is mainly responsible.
